name: BTC 4h Retest Hold Timeout v1
edge_mechanism: a break from range is not chased; price must retest and hold the broken level, and the trade is de-risked by time if expansion does not appear quickly
thesis: BTC 4h is the best-fit lane in the basket for retest-and-hold continuation with a time-based fail-safe. In a chop-dominant environment, first breaks are unreliable, but accepted retests can still work if the trade exits quickly when expansion fails to arrive.
invalidation_condition: If retests remain too sparse, if the hold rarely survives the next bar, or if the time-based de-risk neutralizes nearly every trade before edge appears, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price leaves a short-term range, retests the broken boundary, holds it, and either expands quickly or is de-risked as a failed transition.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Chop-to-transition states often fake the first move but can still reward delayed acceptance entries. The time-based de-risk is essential because real transitions should move soon after the retest hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is a better fit than ETH 4h for this concept because it is still choppy but carries more transition tendency and cleaner structure around range breaks.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may remain stuck in range, causing retest entries to arrive on weak pseudo-breaks that never transition.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but too many become scratches, strengthen the retest hold condition before loosening the breakout boundary.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Moderate number of trades, several fast de-risks, and a smaller set of clean transitions that produce most of the payoff.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h produces enough accepted retests, time-based de-risk contains false transitions, and at least one cross-lane preserves the same logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or timeout behavior preventing any real edge from expressing.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but materially denser than the killed breakout-persistence families

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for retest-and-hold continuation with time-based de-risk.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough accepted retests and the timeout contains failed transitions.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h remains too range-bound and retests never transition.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with many small scratches and a few strong transition winners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same retest-hold mechanism survives on the cycle's primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves accepted retests with cleaner but smaller transitions.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms that retest continuation is simply too sparse in pure chop.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner retests than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same retest-hold logic becomes more explosive on a higher-amplitude lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h preserves retest acceptance with enough structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h turns the concept into late-entry noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, larger expansion legs than BTC.

long: De-risk to break-even if no 0.30 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars
short: De-risk to break-even if no 0.30 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars
